title: Variadic Integer Sum
prompt_md:
## Goal

Implement a C-style variadic function

```cpp
int sumInts(int count, ...)
```

that returns the sum of the `count` `int` values passed after `count`. This is the lesson's core skill: pulling arguments out of the `...` pack with the `<cstdarg>` tools. The `main` below already reads the input and forwards the values to `sumInts` for each supported count -- you only need to write the function body.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 <= n <= 5), how many values follow.
- Line 2: `n` space-separated integers (each between -1000 and 1000).

## Output

- The sum of the `n` values, on one line.

title: Variadic Minimum Finder
prompt_md:
## Goal

Implement a C-style variadic function

```cpp
int findMin(int count, ...)
```

that returns the smallest of the `count` `int` values passed after `count`, using the `<cstdarg>` tools to read the argument pack. The `main` below already reads input and forwards the values for each supported count -- write only the function body.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 <= n <= 5).
- Line 2: `n` space-separated integers (each between -10000 and 10000).

## Output

- The minimum of the `n` values, on one line.
